scope: AgenC runtime/src/tui/ink/ ↔ openclaude src/ink/ wholesale-copy parity. Every common file is openclaude byte-equivalent after the transformation rules below; every AgenC-only file has an explicit row justifying why it exists. No other deviations are allowed; the gate fails on any unjustified drift.
transformationRules: import-rewire: '../utils/<m>.js' (top) / '../../utils/<m>.js' (subdir) → vendored/* analogous, only for modules listed in vendored/ (debug, earlyInput, env, envUtils, execFileNoThrow, fullscreen, intl, log, semver, sliceAnsi, state, tempfile)
extension-normalization: relative imports with .ts / .tsx → .js (AgenC ESM convention); .d.ts preserved

brand-scrub: 'Claude Code' / 'Open Claude' / 'OpenClaude' → 'AgenC'; 'claude' (word, not before -) → 'agenc'; '~/.claude/' → '~/.agenc/'; 'openclaude-clipboard' → 'agenc-clipboard'; internal Anthropic Slack URLs → '(internal reference)'; 'rgb(215,119,87) (AgenC)' → 'rgb(215,119,87)'
ts-expect-error-relaxation: '@ts-expect-error' → '@ts-ignore' (openclaude's tsconfig is looser; AgenC's strict checks make many directives 'unused')
logForDebugging-import: when a file uses logForDebugging without importing it (openclaude bug pattern), AgenC adds the import from vendored/debug.js

requiredBehaviors: AgenC vendored/* directory provides shims for openclaude's outside-tree imports (src/bootstrap/state, src/utils/{debug,earlyInput,env,envUtils,execFileNoThrow,fullscreen,intl,log,semver,sliceAnsi,tempfile}, src/native-ts/yoga-layout)
vendored/yoga-layout/{enums,index}.ts is the same pure-TypeScript Yoga port openclaude itself ships at src/native-ts/yoga-layout (both projects use this port; no WASM swap)
every transformed openclaude-side import targeting one of those modules resolves to runtime/src/tui/ink/vendored/<module>.js
vendored/* files are AgenC-side build glue and intentionally do NOT mirror an openclaude src/ink/ file
AgenC ambient.d.ts contains TS ambient declarations for the vendored runtime deps (react-reconciler, bidi-js, Bun) that openclaude resolves via @types or its own bundler config
this file is intentionally AgenC-only with no openclaude equivalent; required by AgenC's stricter typecheck
compiler-only — not imported at runtime

AgenC global.d.ts declares ink-link, ink-progress, ink-raw-ansi intrinsic JSX elements that openclaude's slimmer global.d.ts omits — required by Link.tsx, RawAnsi.tsx, and any caller above ink/ that renders these elements
AgenC also declares the React 19 module form (declare module 'react' { namespace JSX { ... } }) so jsx-runtime resolves intrinsics on both legacy and React 19 paths
wholesale-port leaves this file untouched (KEEP_AGENC_VERSION list in /tmp/wholesale-port.mjs)

openclaude src/ink/events/event-handlers.ts imports type { PasteEvent } from './paste-event.js' and type { ResizeEvent } from './resize-event.js' but openclaude does NOT ship those files in src/ink/events/
AgenC supplies events/paste-event.ts and events/resize-event.ts as the missing type definitions so the (otherwise wholesale-copied) event-handlers.ts compiles
this is missing-upstream-file adaptation, not AgenC divergence — events/resize-event.ts has the same justification (locked by row missing-upstream-event-types-resize)
AgenC supplies events/resize-event.ts as the missing ResizeEvent type referenced by openclaude's wholesale-copied event-handlers.ts (paired with paste-event.ts; see missing-upstream-event-types row)
openclaude src/ink/termio/{parser,sgr,esc,osc}.ts import './types.js' and './tokenize.js' but openclaude does NOT ship those files
AgenC supplies termio/types.ts (shared termio enums/interfaces) and termio/tokenize.ts (SGR/parameter tokenization helpers) so the (wholesale-copied) parser/sgr/esc/osc compile
missing-upstream-file adaptation; locks the AgenC-supplied type and tokenize modules
openclaude src/ink/frame.ts imports type { Cursor } from './cursor.js' but openclaude does NOT ship that file
AgenC supplies cursor.ts as the missing Cursor type definition; module is type-only and emits no runtime code
missing-upstream-file adaptation

AgenC StdoutContext provides the React context object the wholesale-ported components consume to read the active stdout stream — needed by AgenC consumers above ink/ (transcript, cockpit) that mount Ink-rendered subtrees and need to inject a non-process.stdout writer
if openclaude's components/App.tsx imports a stdout context it lives outside src/ink/components/; AgenC pulls the equivalent into ink/components/ to keep the renderer self-contained
AgenC-extension adaptation; verified: callers exist in runtime/src/tui/cockpit/ and runtime/src/tui/transcript/
openclaude inlines patchConsoleForInk + patchStderrForInk inside ink.tsx (~lines 1601-1675); AgenC extracts them into a sibling console-patch.ts module that ink.tsx imports
the extracted module is byte-equivalent to openclaude's inline implementation (after transformation rules) — code is identical, only the file boundary differs
extraction is documented refactor; no behavioral change
alternative: fold the logic back inline and delete console-patch.ts — open-question for future cleanup, not required here

AgenC-only test defending four real Ink-renderer concerns that survive the wholesale port: declared-cursor cleared on unmount, stale terminal-focus reset before fresh mount, alt-screen scroll mode bracketing on alt-screen lifecycle, raw/input mode reassert on SIGCONT
tests are AgenC-extension safety nets sitting on top of openclaude's test set; openclaude does not have an equivalent file
AgenC-only stress test defending long-transcript-history stability under combined streaming+overlay+resize+selection+scroll load

the original AgenC-only selection-drag-coalescing and onInputActivity sub-tests were already removed (defended now-removed AgenC-only behavior); the file's remaining tests cover behaviors that survive the wholesale port

AgenC-only test guarding the incremental-diff path against accidental full-reset emission — locks behavior of the openclaude renderer that AgenC depends on, expressed at the AgenC integration boundary
openclaude has no equivalent test file at this scope
AgenC-only test defending the I-70 invariant the file is named after
openclaude does not have an equivalent file; the test sits at the AgenC integration boundary
AgenC-only test exercising the vendored yoga-layout port (runtime/src/tui/ink/vendored/yoga-layout/) at the contract level — locks layout primitives the renderer depends on
openclaude does not ship an equivalent test, but its yoga-port is the same source-of-truth and benefits indirectly

openclaude tsconfig is looser than AgenC's. Four discrete strictness mismatches require explicit fixes after the wholesale copy: (a) Button.tsx useRef(null) → useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>|null>(null), (b) ink.tsx 'production' === 'development' → ('production' as string) === 'development' to silence TS2367, (c) ink.tsx stderr-intercept callback signature widened from Error|undefined to Error|null|undefined to match Node.js types, (d) render-node-to-output.ts (candidate.nodeName as string) !== '#text' to silence TS2367
each fix is a pure type annotation; no runtime behavior change
fixes are scripted in /tmp/wholesale-port.mjs (Button.tsx useRef pattern) or applied by hand (the three remaining)
/tmp/wholesale-port.mjs is the canonical port script that applies the transformation rules above
running the script against openclaude src/ink/ and writing into runtime/src/tui/ink/ must be IDEMPOTENT — re-running on already-ported code produces the same result (no double-rewriting)
the script's KEEP_AGENC_VERSION list documents files the script does not overwrite (currently global.d.ts only)
the matrix gate (scripts/check-openclaude-ink-wholesale-parity.mjs) verifies post-transform parity between openclaude and AgenC for every file not on KEEP_AGENC_VERSION list
